phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 50.3 dBA, gradually settling to 32.3-38.3 dBA midway through session. Speech event detected at 416s mark with confidence 0.59. Overall environment is exceptionally quiet, highly conducive to concentration.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 60 bpm throughout session (baseline typical for calm, focused state). Data indicates user was in optimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: Location services recorded 3.2m initial error that converges to 1.3m by session midpoint. Accelerometer detects ongoing adjustments and repositioning throughout the recording.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) diverges from kinetic patterns. Acoustic consistency observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session exemplifies near-optimal conditions: minimal acoustic disturbance (38.3 dB), variable positioning, and strong focus indicators. Duration (423s) sufficient for sustained concentration assessment.

phase_3.recommendations: Address underlying restlessness through movement breaks between sessions.

reason: Concentration score reflects excellent audio conditions (38.3dB), frequent motion, and adequate session length.
